messages.content: In my opinion, using human subjects for medical research without informed consent isn't right, even if it could save lives. Trust and respect for the individual come first.
No

### Reply to Elizabeth Cross' Comment ###
I understand where you're coming from but I think we need to prioritize advancing medical knowledge too. Ethics and respect absolutely mean informed consent must be given freely and fully understood by participants in any medical study. So my stance is definitively yes, ethical medical research requires informed consent always.

Informed consent is non-negotiable in any form of research. Ensuring participants truly understand what they're agreeing to. It's about respecting their autonomy not just ticking boxes. #MedicalEthics #InformedConsent
The potential benefits shouldn't overshadow the fundamental right to know and consent. Not even close to something okay with me.
### Yes
It’s crucial that individuals make informed decisions about their own bodies. We need to protect patients and set strong ethical standards.
No

### Personal Opinon on Medical Ethics

It's not ethical to use human subjects for medical research without their informed consent. Each individual deserves to make an educated decision about their participation. Consent is the cornerstone of trust and dignity. Yes, always Yes. ✍️🌐🧬
So true, Pete. When there's uncertainty, ensuring informed consent is vital. It builds trust and respects our autonomy. Thanks for standing firm on this. 💯📝👩‍⚕️

### Opinion on Medical Research

Ethics in medical research is no joke. Knowing and agreeing to be part of a study shows respect and value for participants. Every soul is autonomous. So yes, definitely Yes. 🆚📖👨‍⚕️
Exactly, David. It’s essential to honor every participant's autonomy. No one should be coerced into any study. Let’s prioritize informed consent. ☑️✍️🌿💡
Strong stance, Matthew. Informed consent isn’t just about research protocols; it’s about treating people with the dignity they deserve. ✨🔗✍️🪫💡
True, Robert. It sets the right foundation for trust and transparency. Every participant should leave knowing exactly what they agreed to. 🔄🔍✅🔒
Great conversation! Ensuring that each participant is fully informed before taking part is crucial. Consent makes all the difference, fostering trust and respect in research processes. 🚀🌟📝✅💖
Absolutely, Allison. It’s the least we can do to empower individuals. Educating them on their rights and the research itself—it’s the building block of ethical conduct. ✨📝💪❤️
Well said, Allison. Education is key—when participants understand what they're getting into, it strengthens the foundation of every study. 📚🔬📍🌟
Indeed, John. It’s all about making informed decisions together. Every step in the right direction fosters trust and respect among researchers and subjects alike. 📝💡➡️👩‍🔬📊💖
